summary.intent: Resolve multiple rounds of Codex review comments on PR #678 in fogodev/ars-ui, specifically addressing splitter component collapse/expand behavior and grid edge cases.
summary.outcome: Fourth-pass Codex review threads were addressed and committed (commit pushing fixes for drag cancellation on mid-drag prop sync, collapse/expand size restoration, zero-column grid guard, and non-collapsible panel expand guard). All tests passed and the PR was pushed with another Codex review triggered.

repo: The repo uses a Codex bot for automated PR reviews; approval is signaled via a '+1' reaction, active review via 'eyes' reaction on the PR issue.
Spec conformance tests live under `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance layout` and must be kept in sync with `spec/components/layout/*.md` files.
Proptest state machines for splitter are run with `--ignored` flag: `cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines proptest_splitter --features i18n -- --ignored`.
Snapshot tests use `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` to enforce no stale snapshots.
The project uses `cargo xtask spec validate` for spec validation and `cargo +nightly fmt --all` for formatting.
Splitter component has a distinction between controlled (observable Bindable) and uncontrolled sizing, and both paths must be kept in sync.
Panel collapse/expand requires remembering pre-collapse sizes for restoration; expand must guard against non-collapsible panels.

code.finding: Drag state and exit_dragging logic; mid-drag prop sync requires cancelling the active drag to avoid desync between observable Bindable and internal shadow sizes.
expand_panel logic; must guard against non-collapsible panels and restore pre-collapse sizes from saved memory rather than computing from current state.
expand_panel function definition; collapse/expand toggle needs a restore-size memory field to correctly round-trip panel sizes.
Grid style output must reject zero column counts (guard on `if let Some(cols)` before emitting grid-template-columns CSS).
Spec snippets for collapse/expand, keyboard resizing, and controlled sizing needed updates to match the new implementation behavior.

workflow: Polling for Codex review verdict via GraphQL `reviewThreads` + REST reactions in a bash loop is the established pattern; loop exits on '+1' reaction or when unresolved Codex threads appear without 'eyes' signal.
After each fix round: run focused unit tests, spec conformance, fmt check, spec validate, proptest, and insta snapshots before committing.
Commits include `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>` and reference the fix commit hash in thread replies for traceability.
Review threads are resolved programmatically via GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation after posting a reply with the fix commit reference.
Triggering a new Codex review pass is done by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment after resolving all current threads.

friction: Fourth review pass took longer than normal (past the quick-review window) while the 'eyes' reaction was active but no threads appeared, requiring defensive continued polling.
Multiple iterative patch rounds were needed on splitter.rs due to interacting concerns (drag cancellation, size restoration, non-collapsible guard) discovered across separate Codex threads.
Spec markdown files required coordinated updates alongside code changes to keep spec conformance tests green.
open_items: A fifth Codex review pass was triggered at the end of the session; its verdict and any new threads are unresolved.
The pattern of needing to cancel in-progress drags on mid-drag prop changes may warrant a more robust controlled/uncontrolled reconciliation strategy in the splitter.
